channel.title: Venture Building on >_ John Januszczak | Technology & Innovation
channel.description: Recent content in Venture Building on >_ John Januszczak | Technology & Innovation

item.title: Investing in Atoms: How AI is Forcing Venture Capital to Break its Software Addiction
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: Venture capital has spent 15 years addicted to the easy returns of software. Now, AI is exposing the scarcity of physical infrastructure, energy, and hardware: the ‘atoms’ that actually power intelligence.

item.title: From Fintech to Energy Abundance: Unpacking My Latest Conversation on Founders Only
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: A deep dive into the transition from building UBX to driving energy abundance in the Philippines with 1882 Energy Ventures.

item.title: The Build vs. Buy of Innovation: CVC vs. Corporate Venture Building
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: Choosing between a CVC and a CVB is a defining strategic decision. Understanding the ROI math—why a $100M exit can be more valuable than a $1B Unicorn—is the key to winning the innovation game.

item.title: Scaling Innovation: Beyond the Theatre of "New"
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: In this deep-dive episode of FINCIRCLE, John Januszczak explores the journey of building and scaling ventures in the fintech space. He shares insights on leadership, the transition from corporate executive to venture builder, and why scaling innovation requires more than just capital—it requires a fundamental shift in mindset and architecture.

item.title: The Bank for the Unbankable
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: The Business Development Bank of Canada is neither a traditional bank nor a typical venture capitalist. Built to serve entrepreneurs rejected by commercial lenders, BDC has quietly become one of the most powerful counter-cyclical institutions in the Canadian economy—and the country’s largest venture capital investor. This is the story of how a government-owned bank learned to profit from market failure while stabilizing innovation, startups, and small businesses through crises.

item.title: The Temporal Architecture of Alpha
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: An overlay on Alex Danco’s ‘Prediction’ thesis, arguing that business strategy is finally moving from a spatial ‘portfolio’ model to a temporal ‘stream’ model.

item.title: Why Our "Free ERP" Failed (& Why AI Changes the Battlefield)
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: We gave Philippine SMEs a free operating system, but they didn’t use it. In this retrospective, I analyze the failure of ERPX through the lens of modern AI architecture and explain why solving the ‘Cost’ barrier wasn’t enough without solving the ‘Effort’ barrier.

item.title: The Final Pitch: Investing in the Heroes of the Digital Economy
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: John Januszczak joins the judges’ panel of The Final Pitch Season 6, sharing his vision for invisible banking and the role of venture studios in building the post-pandemic digital economy.

item.title: Path to Scale
item.author: info@januszczak.org (John Januszczak)
item.description: Strategies for achieving exponential growth in the fintech ecosystem.
